  35. 35No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood –
  36. 36[As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,
  37. 37[From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.
  38. 38The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.
  39. 39That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.
  40. 40Indeed, We have warned you of a near punishment on the Day when a man will observe what his hands have put forth and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish that I were dust!"
Sūrah 79

an-Nāziʿāt

Those Who Pull Out · 46 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By those [angels] who extract with violence
  2. 2And [by] those who remove with ease
  3. 3And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming
  4. 4And those who race each other in a race
  5. 5And those who arrange [each] matter,
  6. 6On the Day the blast [of the Horn] will convulse [creation],
  7. 7There will follow it the subsequent [one].
  8. 8Hearts, that Day, will tremble,
  9. 9Their eyes humbled.
  10. 10They are [presently] saying, "Will we indeed be returned to [our] former state [of life]?
  11. 11Even if we should be decayed bones?"
  12. 12They say, "That, then, would be a losing return."
  13. 13Indeed, it will be but one shout,
  14. 14And suddenly they will be [alert] upon the earth's surface.
  15. 15Has there reached you the story of Moses? –
  16. 16When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā,
  17. 17"Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.
  18. 18And say to him, 'Would you [be willing to] purify yourself
  19. 19And let me guide you to your Lord so you would fear [Him]?' "
  20. 20And he showed him the greatest sign,
  21. 21But he [i.e., Pharaoh] denied and disobeyed.
  22. 22Then he turned his back, striving [i.e., plotting].
  23. 23And he gathered [his people] and called out
  24. 24And said, "I am your most exalted lord."
  25. 25So Allāh seized him in exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression].
  26. 26Indeed in that is a lesson [i.e., warning] for whoever would fear [Allāh].
  27. 27Are you a more difficult creation or is the heaven? He [i.e., Allāh] constructed it.
  28. 28He raised its ceiling and proportioned it.
  29. 29And He darkened its night and extracted its brightness.
  30. 30And after that He spread the earth.
  31. 31He extracted from it its water and its pasture,
  32. 32And the mountains He set firmly
  33. 33As enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.
  34. 34But when there comes the greatest Overwhelming Calamity –
  35. 35The Day when man will remember that for which he strove,
  36. 36And Hellfire will be exposed for [all] those who see –
  37. 37So as for he who transgressed
  38. 38And preferred the life of the world,
  39. 39Then indeed, Hellfire will be [his] refuge.
  40. 40But as for he who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from [unlawful] inclination,
  41. 41Then indeed, Paradise will be [his] refuge.
  42. 42They ask you, [O Muḥammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival?
  43. 43In what [position] are you that you should mention it?
  44. 44To your Lord is its finality.
  45. 45You are only a warner for those who fear it.
  46. 46It will be, on the Day they see it, as though they had not remained [in the world] except for an afternoon or a morning thereof.
Sūrah 80

ʿAbasa

He Frowned · 42 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1He [i.e., the Prophet ()] frowned and turned away
  2. 2Because there came to him the blind man, [interrupting].
  3. 3But what would make you perceive, [O Muḥammad], that perhaps he might be purified
  4. 4Or be reminded and the remembrance would benefit him?
  5. 5As for he who thinks himself without need,
  6. 6To him you give attention.
  7. 7And not upon you [is any blame] if he will not be purified.
  8. 8But as for he who came to you striving [for knowledge]
  9. 9While he fears [Allāh],
  10. 10From him you are distracted.
  11. 11No! Indeed, they [i.e., these verses] are a reminder;
  12. 12So whoever wills may remember it.
  13. 13[It is recorded] in honored sheets,
  14. 14Exalted and purified,
  15. 15[Carried] by the hands of messenger-angels,
  16. 16Noble and dutiful.
  17. 17Destroyed [i.e., cursed] is man; how disbelieving is he.
  18. 18From what thing [i.e., substance] did He create him?
  19. 19From a sperm-drop He created him and destined for him;
  20. 20Then He eased the way for him;
  21. 21Then He causes his death and provides a grave for him.
  22. 22Then when He wills, He will resurrect him.
  23. 23No! He [i.e., man] has not yet accomplished what He commanded him.
  24. 24Then let mankind look at his food –
  25. 25How We poured down water in torrents,
  26. 26Then We broke open the earth, splitting [it with sprouts],
  27. 27And caused to grow within it grain
  28. 28And grapes and herbage
  29. 29And olive and palm trees
  30. 30And gardens of dense shrubbery
  31. 31And fruit and grass –
  32. 32[As] enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.
  33. 33But when there comes the Deafening Blast
  34. 34On the Day a man will flee from his brother
  35. 35And his mother and his father
  36. 36And his wife and his children,
  37. 37For every man, that Day, will be a matter adequate for him.
  38. 38[Some] faces, that Day, will be bright –
  39. 39Laughing, rejoicing at good news.
  40. 40And [other] faces, that Day, will have upon them dust.
  41. 41Blackness will cover them.
  42. 42Those are the disbelievers, the wicked ones.
Sūrah 81

at-Takweer

The Folding Up · 29 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1When the sun is wrapped up [in darkness]
  2. 2And when the stars fall, dispersing,
  3. 3And when the mountains are removed
  4. 4And when full-term she-camels are neglected
  5. 5And when the wild beasts are gathered
  6. 6And when the seas are filled with flame
  7. 7And when the souls are paired
  8. 8And when the girl [who was] buried alive is asked
  9. 9For what sin she was killed
  10. 10And when the pages are spread [i.e., made public]
  11. 11And when the sky is stripped away
  12. 12And when Hellfire is set ablaze
  13. 13And when Paradise is brought near,
  14. 14A soul will [then] know what it has brought [with it].
  15. 15So I swear by the retreating stars –
  16. 16Those that run [their courses] and disappear [i.e., set] –
  17. 17And by the night as it closes in
  18. 18And by the dawn when it breathes [i.e., stirs]
  19. 19[That] indeed, it [i.e., the Qurʾān] is a word [conveyed by] a noble messenger [i.e., Gabriel]
  20. 20[Who is] possessed of power and with the Owner of the Throne, secure [in position],
  21. 21Obeyed there [in the heavens] and trustworthy.
  22. 22And your companion [i.e., Prophet Muḥammad ()] is not [at all] mad.
  23. 23And he has already seen him [i.e., Gabriel] in the clear horizon.
  24. 24And he [i.e., Muḥammad ()] i s not a withholder of [knowledge of] the unseen.
  25. 25And it [i.e., the Qurʾān] is not the word of a devil, expelled [from the heavens].
  26. 26So where are you going?
  27. 27It is not except a reminder to the worlds
  28. 28For whoever wills among you to take a right course.
  29. 29And you do not will except that Allāh wills – Lord of the worlds.
Sūrah 82

al-Infiṭār

The Cleaving · 19 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1When the sky breaks apart
  2. 2And when the stars fall, scattering,
  3. 3And when the seas are erupted
  4. 4And when the [contents of] graves are scattered [i.e., exposed],
  5. 5A soul will [then] know what it has put forth and kept back.
  6. 6O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous,
  7. 7Who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you?
  8. 8In whatever form He willed has He assembled you.
  9. 9No! But you deny the Recompense.
  10. 10And indeed, [appointed] over you are keepers,
  11. 11Noble and recording;
  12. 12They know whatever you do.
  13. 13Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure,
  14. 14And indeed, the wicked will be in Hellfire.
  15. 15They will [enter to] burn therein on the Day of Recompense,
  16. 16And never therefrom will they be absent.
  17. 17And what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?
  18. 18Then, what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?
  19. 19It is the Day when a soul will not possess for another soul [power to do] a thing; and the command, that Day, is [entirely] with Allāh.
Sūrah 83

al-Muṭaffifeen

The Defrauders · 36 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Woe to those who give less [than due],
  2. 2Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.
  3. 3But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.
  4. 4Do they not think that they will be resurrected
  5. 5For a tremendous Day –
  6. 6The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?
  7. 7No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.
  8. 8And what can make you know what is sijjeen?
  9. 9It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.
  10. 10Woe, that Day, to the deniers,
  11. 11Who deny the Day of Recompense.
  12. 12And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.
  13. 13When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
  14. 14No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.
  15. 15No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.
  16. 16Then indeed, they will [enter and] burn in Hellfire.
  17. 17Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."
  18. 18No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in ʿilliyyūn.
  19. 19And what can make you know what is ʿilliyyūn?
  20. 20It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed
  21. 21Which is witnessed by those brought near [to Allāh].
  22. 22Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure
  23. 23On adorned couches, observing.
  24. 24You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.
  25. 25They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.
  26. 26The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.
  27. 27And its mixture is of Tasneem,
  28. 28A spring from which those near [to Allāh] drink.
  29. 29Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.
  30. 30And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.
  31. 31And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.
  32. 32And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."
  33. 33But they had not been sent as guardians over them.
  34. 34So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,
  35. 35On adorned couches, observing.
  36. 36Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?
Sūrah 84

al-Inshiqāq

The Splitting Open · 25 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1When the sky has split [open]
  2. 2And has listened [i.e., responded] to its Lord and was obligated [to do so]
  3. 3And when the earth has been extended
  4. 4And has cast out that within it and relinquished [it]
  5. 5And has listened [i.e., responded] to its Lord and was obligated [to do so] –
  6. 6O mankind, indeed you are laboring toward your Lord with [great] exertion and will meet it.
  7. 7Then as for he who is given his record in his right hand,
  8. 8He will be judged with an easy account
  9. 9And return to his people in happiness.
  10. 10But as for he who is given his record behind his back,
  11. 11He will cry out for destruction
  12. 12And [enter to] burn in a Blaze.
  13. 13Indeed, he had [once] been among his people in happiness;
  14. 14Indeed, he had thought he would never return [to Allāh].
  15. 15But yes! Indeed, his Lord was ever, of him, Seeing.
  16. 16So I swear by the twilight glow
  17. 17And [by] the night and what it envelops
  18. 18And [by] the moon when it becomes full
  19. 19[That] you will surely embark upon [i.e., experience] state after state.
  20. 20So what is [the matter] with them [that] they do not believe,
  21. 21And when the Qurʾān is recited to them, they do not prostrate [to Allāh]?
  22. 22But those who have disbelieved deny,
  23. 23And Allāh is most knowing of what they keep within themselves.
  24. 24So give them tidings of a painful punishment,
  25. 25Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them is a reward uninterrupted.
Sūrah 85

al-Burūj

The Mansions of the Stars · 22 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the sky containing great stars
  2. 2And [by] the promised Day
  3. 3And [by] the witness and what is witnessed,
  4. 4Destroyed [i.e., cursed] were the companions of the trench
  5. 5[Containing] the fire full of fuel,
  6. 6When they were sitting near it
  7. 7And they, to what they were doing against the believers, were witnesses.
  8. 8And they resented them not except because they believed in Allāh, the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy,
  9. 9To whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And Allāh, over all things, is Witness.
  10. 10Indeed, those who have tortured the believing men and believing women and then have not repented will have the punishment of Hell, and they will have the punishment of the Burning Fire.
  11. 11Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds will have gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great attainment.
  12. 12Indeed, the assault [i.e., vengeance] of your Lord is severe.
  13. 13Indeed, it is He who originates [creation] and repeats.
  14. 14And He is the Forgiving, the Affectionate,
  15. 15Honorable Owner of the Throne,
  16. 16Effecter of what He intends.
  17. 17Has there reached you the story of the soldiers –
  18. 18[Those of] Pharaoh and Thamūd?
  19. 19But they who disbelieve are in [persistent] denial,
  20. 20While Allāh encompasses them from behind.
  21. 21But this is an honored Qurʾān
  22. 22[Inscribed] in a Preserved Slate.
Sūrah 86

aṭ-Ṭāriq

The Night-Comer · 17 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the sky and the night comer –
  2. 2And what can make you know what is the night comer?
  3. 3It is the piercing star –
  4. 4There is no soul but that it has over it a protector.
  5. 5So let man observe from what he was created.
  6. 6He was created from a fluid, ejected,
  7. 7Emerging from between the backbone and the ribs.
  8. 8Indeed, He [i.e., Allāh], to return him [to life], is Able.
  9. 9The Day when secrets will be put on trial,
  10. 10Then he [i.e., man] will have no power or any helper.
  11. 11By the sky which returns [rain]
  12. 12And [by] the earth which cracks open,
  13. 13Indeed, it [i.e., the Qurʾān] is a decisive statement,
  14. 14And it is not amusement.
  15. 15Indeed, they are planning a plan,
  16. 16But I am planning a plan.
  17. 17So allow time for the disbelievers. Leave them awhile.
Sūrah 87

al-Aʿlā

The Most High · 19 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High,
  2. 2Who created and proportioned
  3. 3And who destined and [then] guided
  4. 4And who brings out the pasture
  5. 5And [then] makes it black stubble.
  6. 6We will make you recite, [O Muḥammad], and you will not forget,
  7. 7Except what Allāh should will. Indeed, He knows what is declared and what is hidden.
  8. 8And We will ease you toward ease.
  9. 9So remind, if the reminder should benefit;
  10. 10He who fears [Allāh] will be reminded.
  11. 11But the wretched one will avoid it –
  12. 12[He] who will [enter and] burn in the greatest Fire,
  13. 13Neither dying therein nor living.
  14. 14He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself
  15. 15And mentions the name of his Lord and prays.
  16. 16But you prefer the worldly life,
  17. 17While the Hereafter is better and more enduring.
  18. 18Indeed, this is in the former scriptures,
  19. 19The scriptures of Abraham and Moses.
Sūrah 88

al-Ghāshiyah

The Overwhelming · 26 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Has there reached you the report of the Overwhelming [event]?
  2. 2[Some] faces, that Day, will be humbled,
  3. 3Working [hard] and exhausted.
  4. 4They will [enter to] burn in an intensely hot Fire.
  5. 5They will be given drink from a boiling spring.
  6. 6For them there will be no food except from a poisonous, thorny plant
  7. 7Which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger.
  8. 8[Other] faces, that Day, will show pleasure.
  9. 9With their effort [they are] satisfied
  10. 10In an elevated garden,
  11. 11Wherein they will hear no unsuitable speech.
  12. 12Within it is a flowing spring.
  13. 13Within it are couches raised high
  14. 14And cups put in place
  15. 15And cushions lined up
  16. 16And carpets spread around.
  17. 17Then do they not look at the camels – how they are created?
  18. 18And at the sky – how it is raised?
  19. 19And at the mountains – how they are erected?
  20. 20And at the earth – how it is spread out?
  21. 21So remind, [O Muḥammad]; you are only a reminder.
  22. 22You are not over them a controller.
  23. 23However, he who turns away and disbelieves –
  24. 24Then Allāh will punish him with the greatest punishment.
  25. 25Indeed, to Us is their return.
  26. 26Then indeed, upon Us is their account.
Sūrah 89

al-Fajr

The Dawn · 30 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the dawn
  2. 2And [by] ten nights
  3. 3And [by] the even [number] and the odd
  4. 4And [by] the night when it passes,
  5. 5Is there [not] in [all] that an oath [sufficient] for one of perception?
  6. 6Have you not considered how your Lord dealt with ʿAad –
  7. 7[With] Iram – who had lofty pillars,
  8. 8The likes of whom had never been created in the land?
  9. 9And [with] Thamūd, who carved out the rocks in the valley?
  10. 10And [with] Pharaoh, owner of the stakes? –
  11. 11[All of] whom oppressed within the lands
  12. 12And increased therein the corruption.
  13. 13So your Lord poured upon them a scourge of punishment.
  14. 14Indeed, your Lord is in observation.
  15. 15And as for man, when his Lord tries him and [thus] is generous to him and favors him, he says, "My Lord has honored me."
  16. 16But when He tries him and restricts his provision, he says, "My Lord has humiliated me."
  17. 17No! But you do not honor the orphan
  18. 18And you do not encourage one another to feed the poor.
  19. 19And you consume inheritance, devouring [it] altogether,
  20. 20And you love wealth with immense love.
  21. 21No! When the earth has been leveled – pounded and crushed –
  22. 22And your Lord has come and the angels, rank upon rank,
  23. 23And brought [within view], that Day, is Hell – that Day, man will remember, but how [i.e., what good] to him will be the remembrance?
  24. 24He will say, "Oh, I wish I had sent ahead [some good] for my life."
  25. 25So on that Day, none will punish [as severely] as His punishment,
  26. 26And none will bind [as severely] as His binding [of the evildoers].
  27. 27[To the righteous it will be said], "O reassured soul,
  28. 28Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him],
  29. 29And enter among My [righteous] servants
  30. 30And enter My Paradise."
Sūrah 90

al-Balad

The City · 20 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1I swear by this city [i.e., Makkah] –
  2. 2And you, [O Muḥammad], are free of restriction in this city –
  3. 3And [by] the father and that which was born [of him],
  4. 4We have certainly created man into hardship.
  5. 5Does he think that never will anyone overcome him?
  6. 6He says, "I have spent wealth in abundance."
  7. 7Does he think that no one has seen him?
  8. 8Have We not made for him two eyes?
  9. 9And a tongue and two lips?
  10. 10And have shown him the two ways?
  11. 11But he has not broken through the difficult pass.
  12. 12And what can make you know what is [breaking through] the difficult pass?
  13. 13It is the freeing of a slave
  14. 14Or feeding on a day of severe hunger
  15. 15An orphan of near relationship
  16. 16Or a needy person in misery
  17. 17And then being among those who believed and advised one another to patience and advised one another to compassion.
  18. 18Those are the companions of the right.
  19. 19But they who disbelieved in Our signs – those are the companions of the left.
  20. 20Over them will be fire closed in.
Sūrah 91

ash-Shams

The Sun · 15 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the sun and its brightness
  2. 2And [by] the moon when it follows it
  3. 3And [by] the day when it displays it
  4. 4And [by] the night when it covers [i.e., conceals] it
  5. 5And [by] the sky and He who constructed it
  6. 6And [by] the earth and He who spread it
  7. 7And [by] the soul and He who proportioned it
  8. 8And inspired it [with discernment of] its wickedness and its righteousness,
  9. 9He has succeeded who purifies it,
  10. 10And he has failed who instills it [with corruption].
  11. 11Thamūd denied [their prophet] by reason of their transgression,
  12. 12When the most wretched of them was sent forth.
  13. 13And the messenger of Allāh [i.e., Ṣāliḥ] said to them, "[Do not harm] the she-camel of Allāh or [prevent her from] her drink."
  14. 14But they denied him and hamstrung her. So their Lord brought down upon them destruction for their sin and made it equal [upon all of them].
  15. 15And He does not fear the consequence thereof.
Sūrah 92

al-Layl

The Night · 21 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the night when it covers
  2. 2And [by] the day when it appears
  3. 3And [by] He who created the male and female,
  4. 4Indeed, your efforts are diverse.
  5. 5As for he who gives and fears Allāh
  6. 6And believes in the best [reward],
  7. 7We will ease him toward ease.
  8. 8But as for he who withholds and considers himself free of need
  9. 9And denies the best [reward],
  10. 10We will ease him toward difficulty.
  11. 11And what will his wealth avail him when he falls?
  12. 12Indeed, [incumbent] upon Us is guidance.
  13. 13And indeed, to Us belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].
  14. 14So I have warned you of a Fire which is blazing.
  15. 15None will [enter to] burn therein except the most wretched one
  16. 16Who had denied and turned away.
  17. 17But the righteous one will avoid it –
  18. 18[He] who gives [from] his wealth to purify himself
  19. 19And not [giving] for anyone who has [done him] a favor to be rewarded
  20. 20But only seeking the countenance of his Lord, Most High.
  21. 21And he is going to be satisfied.
Sūrah 93

aḍ-Ḍuḥā

The Morning Hours · 11 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the morning brightness
  2. 2And [by] the night when it covers with darkness,
  3. 3Your Lord has not taken leave of you, [O Muḥammad], nor has He detested [you].
  4. 4And the Hereafter is better for you than the first [life].
  5. 5And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
  6. 6Did He not find you an orphan and give [you] refuge?
  7. 7And He found you lost and guided [you],
  8. 8And He found you poor and made [you] self-sufficient.
  9. 9So as for the orphan, do not oppress [him].
  10. 10And as for the petitioner, do not repel [him].
  11. 11But as for the favor of your Lord, report [it].
Sūrah 94

ash-Sharḥ

The Opening Forth · 8 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Did We not expand for you, [O Muḥammad], your breast?
  2. 2And We removed from you your burden
  3. 3Which had weighed upon your back
  4. 4And raised high for you your repute.
  5. 5For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease [i.e., relief].
  6. 6Indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
  7. 7So when you have finished [your duties], then stand up [for worship].
  8. 8And to your Lord direct [your] longing.
Sūrah 95

at-Teen

The Fig · 8 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the fig and the olive
  2. 2And [by] Mount Sinai
  3. 3And [by] this secure city [i.e., Makkah],
  4. 4We have certainly created man in the best of stature;
  5. 5Then We return him to the lowest of the low,
  6. 6Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds, for they will have a reward uninterrupted.
  7. 7So what yet causes you to deny the Recompense?
  8. 8Is not Allāh the most just of judges?
Sūrah 96

al-ʿAlaq

The Clot · 19 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Recite in the name of your Lord who created –
  2. 2Created man from a clinging substance.
  3. 3Recite, and your Lord is the most Generous –
  4. 4Who taught by the pen –
  5. 5Taught man that which he knew not.
  6. 6No! [But] indeed, man transgresses
  7. 7Because he sees himself self-sufficient.
  8. 8Indeed, to your Lord is the return.
  9. 9Have you seen the one who forbids
  10. 10A servant when he prays?
  11. 11Have you seen if he is upon guidance
  12. 12Or enjoins righteousness?
  13. 13Have you seen if he denies and turns away –
  14. 14Does he not know that Allāh sees?
  15. 15No! If he does not desist, We will surely drag him by the forelock –
  16. 16A lying, sinning forelock.
  17. 17Then let him call his associates;
  18. 18We will call the angels of Hell.
  19. 19No! Do not obey him. But prostrate and draw near [to Allāh].
Sūrah 97

al-Qadr

The Decree · 5 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Indeed, We sent it [i.e., the Qurʾān] down during the Night of Decree.
  2. 2And what can make you know what is the Night of Decree?
  3. 3The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.
  4. 4The angels and the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] descend therein by permission of their Lord for every matter.
  5. 5Peace it is until the emergence of dawn.
Sūrah 98

al-Bayyinah

The Clear Evidence · 8 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Those who disbelieved among the People of the Scripture and the polytheists were not to be parted [from misbelief] until there came to them clear evidence –
  2. 2A Messenger from Allāh, reciting purified scriptures
  3. 3Within which are correct writings [i.e., rulings and laws].
  4. 4Nor did those who were given the Scripture become divided until after there had come to them clear evidence.
  5. 5And they were not commanded except to worship Allāh, [being] sincere to Him in religion, inclining to truth, and to establish prayer and to give zakāh. And that is the correct religion.
  6. 6Indeed, they who disbelieved among the People of the Scripture and the polytheists will be in the fire of Hell, abiding eternally therein. Those are the worst of creatures.
  7. 7Indeed, they who have believed and done righteous deeds – those are the best of creatures.
  8. 8Their reward with Allāh will be gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever, Allāh being pleased with them and they with Him. That is for whoever has feared his Lord.
Sūrah 99

az-Zalzalah

The Earthquake · 8 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1When the earth is shaken with its [final] earthquake
  2. 2And the earth discharges its burdens
  3. 3And man says, "What is [wrong] with it?" –
  4. 4That Day, it will report its news
  5. 5Because your Lord has inspired [i.e., commanded] it.
  6. 6That Day, the people will depart separated [into categories] to be shown [the result of] their deeds.
  7. 7So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it,
  8. 8And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.
Sūrah 100

al-ʿĀdiyāt

The Runners · 11 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By the racers, panting,
  2. 2And the producers of sparks [when] striking
  3. 3And the chargers at dawn,
  4. 4Stirring up thereby [clouds of] dust,
  5. 5Arriving thereby in the center collectively,
  6. 6Indeed mankind, to his Lord, is ungrateful.
  7. 7And indeed, he is to that a witness.
  8. 8And indeed he is, in love of wealth, intense.
  9. 9But does he not know that when the contents of the graves are scattered
  10. 10And that within the breasts is obtained,
  11. 11Indeed, their Lord with them, that Day, is [fully] Acquainted.
Sūrah 101

al-Qāriʿah

The Striking Hour · 11 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1The Striking Calamity –
  2. 2What is the Striking Calamity?
  3. 3And what can make you know what is the Striking Calamity?
  4. 4It is the Day when people will be like moths, dispersed,
  5. 5And the mountains will be like wool, fluffed up.
  6. 6Then as for one whose scales are heavy [with good deeds],
  7. 7He will be in a pleasant life.
  8. 8But as for one whose scales are light,
  9. 9His refuge will be an abyss.
  10. 10And what can make you know what that is?
  11. 11It is a Fire, intensely hot.
Sūrah 102

at-Takāthur

The Piling Up · 8 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Competition in [worldly] increase diverts you
  2. 2Until you visit the graveyards.
  3. 3No! You are going to know.
  4. 4Then, no! You are going to know.
  5. 5No! If you only knew with knowledge of certainty...
  6. 6You will surely see the Hellfire.
  7. 7Then you will surely see it with the eye of certainty.
  8. 8Then you will surely be asked that Day about pleasure.
Sūrah 103

al-ʿAṣr

The Time · 3 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1By time,
  2. 2Indeed, mankind is in loss,
  3. 3Except for those who have believed and done righteous deeds and advised each other to truth and advised each other to patience.
Sūrah 104

al-Humazah

The Slanderer · 9 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Woe to every scorner and mocker
  2. 2Who collects wealth and [continuously] counts it.
  3. 3He thinks that his wealth will make him immortal.
  4. 4No! He will surely be thrown into the Crusher.
  5. 5And what can make you know what is the Crusher?
  6. 6It is the fire of Allāh, [eternally] fueled,
  7. 7Which mounts directed at the hearts.
  8. 8Indeed, it [i.e., Hellfire] will be closed down upon them
  9. 9In extended columns.
Sūrah 105

al-Feel

The Elephant · 5 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Have you not considered, [O Muḥammad], how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant?
  2. 2Did He not make their plan into misguidance?
  3. 3And He sent against them birds in flocks,
  4. 4Striking them with stones of hard clay,
  5. 5And He made them like eaten straw.
Sūrah 106

Quraysh

Quraysh · 4 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1For the accustomed security of the Quraysh –
  2. 2Their accustomed security [in] the caravan of winter and summer –
  3. 3Let them worship the Lord of this House,
  4. 4Who has fed them, [saving them] from hunger and made them safe, [saving them] from fear.
Sūrah 107

al-Māʿūn

Small Kindnesses · 7 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Have you seen the one who denies the Recompense?
  2. 2For that is the one who drives away the orphan
  3. 3And does not encourage the feeding of the poor.
  4. 4So woe to those who pray
  5. 5[But] who are heedless of their prayer –
  6. 6Those who make show [of their deeds]
  7. 7And withhold [simple] assistance.
Sūrah 108

al-Kawthar

Abundance · 3 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Indeed, We have granted you, [O Muḥammad], al-Kawthar.
  2. 2So pray to your Lord and sacrifice [to Him alone].
  3. 3Indeed, your enemy is the one cut off.
Sūrah 109

al-Kāfirūn

The Disbelievers · 6 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Say, "O disbelievers,
  2. 2I do not worship what you worship.
  3. 3Nor are you worshippers of what I worship.
  4. 4Nor will I be a worshipper of what you worship.
  5. 5Nor will you be worshippers of what I worship.
  6. 6For you is your religion, and for me is my religion."
Sūrah 110

an-Naṣr

The Help · 3 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1When the victory of Allāh has come and the conquest,
  2. 2And you see the people entering into the religion of Allāh in multitudes,
  3. 3Then exalt [Him] with praise of your Lord and ask forgiveness of Him. Indeed, He is ever Accepting of repentance.
Sūrah 111

al-Masad

The Palm Fibre · 5 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1May the hands of Abū Lahab be ruined, and ruined is he.
  2. 2His wealth will not avail him or that which he gained.
  3. 3He will [enter to] burn in a Fire of [blazing] flame
  4. 4And his wife [as well] – the carrier of firewood.
  5. 5Around her neck is a rope of [twisted] fiber.
Sūrah 112

al-Ikhlāṣ

The Sincerity · 4 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Say, "He is Allāh, [who is] One,
  2. 2Allāh, the Eternal Refuge.
  3. 3He neither begets nor is born,
  4. 4Nor is there to Him any equivalent."
Sūrah 113

al-Falaq

The Daybreak · 5 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak
  2. 2From the evil of that which He created
  3. 3And from the evil of darkness when it settles
  4. 4And from the evil of the blowers in knots
  5. 5And from the evil of an envier when he envies."
Sūrah 114

an-Nās

Mankind · 6 verses
Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥeem
  1. 1Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind,
  2. 2The Sovereign of mankind,
  3. 3The God of mankind,
  4. 4From the evil of the retreating whisperer –
  5. 5Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind –
  6. 6From among the jinn and mankind."

Source: Saheeh International (1997, 2004 — Abul-Qasim Publishing House / Al-Muntada Al-Islami). Quoted here under fair use / fair dealing for the purposes of criticism, review, and commentary.
